title: On the Art of Discontent
author: From: Willa Cather
content: The end is nothing; the road is all. If you are not finding a way to enjoy the struggle, you are missing the point of the work.

title: The Framing Bias
author: From: Daniel Kahneman
content: How a choice is presented determines the choice made. Are you framing your progress as a 'gain' or as a 'avoided loss'?

title: The Gambler’s Fallacy
author: From: Probability Theory
content: Believing that 'luck' is due because you've had a bad streak is a trap. Every event is statistically independent.

title: The Law of Least Effort
author: From: Cognitive Science
content: If a system is hard to use, it won't be used. Friction is the enemy of consistency.

title: The Observer Effect
author: From: Quantum Physics
content: You cannot watch a process without influencing it. Your presence on the team changes the team.
